transcript:
Hello everyone, my name is Jay, and I'm a product manager working at Hashed and ShardLab, and I'm here to talk about the x402 and the future of Agentic Economy.

Now Hashed has a stablecoin initiative and that starts with Hashed investing in stablecoin projects like Bastion and Ethena. And at the bottom of the slide we have Hashed Open Research doing deep down level in-depth research into stablecoins. And us in the middle, ShardLab, as a product arm of Hashed, developing real world stablecoin use cases and applications.

And the stablecoin agenda actually expands to x402 because maybe in five years or ten years, we're expecting us to interact with AI agents rather than just web UI. We can think of a desert trip in Abu Dhabi, a package tour that I want to get, and to search for that information I'll be interacting with AI agents sending over policies of maybe hundred dollar budget, location and date. And the rest will come along with the AI agent giving you the simple voucher code and a receipt with the cost that was made along the way.

Now when we talk about x402 at Hashed, the focus is not only about stablecoins but also about AI agents as with the aforementioned user flow. So here at Hashed and ShardLab, we believe the future is more about the agent to agent interface, not just stablecoins.

And as ShardLab as a product arm of Hashed, we are approaching the x402 and AI agent space with two main theses and approaches. First one being, uh, we believe for AI agents a better infrastructure is rather blockchain rails not just traditional finance rails because agents wouldn't have their own identities just like us humans do. And as well, they don't have to be binded by just rule-based and inflexible rules, and as well as they don't have to be binded by settlement speeds like one month subscription period.

And besides that, we also believe that because we don't know what AI agents are capable of doing right now, we need to build a real world applications to understand what they truly are. So we have built an agentic payment module as well as a trading application.

So the first agent payment stablecoin module leverages the KRW stablecoin and 8004 inspired reputation module on top of Solana as well as a compliance engine because we're dealing with a local currency. As well as we also enable other AI agents to interact with our AI agents by giving them along a passport-like attestation.

And this is a simple demo. So here, a user would type in a white pair of sneakers because they wanted to do training, the AI agent would interact with other AI agents to get SKU data. And then we'll narrow it down and filter it with Nike brands and then the user would make a choice and pay with KRW in our case. And with the x402 infrastructure going on, it will settle on Solana within 400 milliseconds of time and you'll be able to manage what the status of delivery is inside of your My Page.

And the second use case is agentic trading. Here, the user just simply funds an AI agent with a customizable strategy and then after that agent just autonomously trades and then agent would also interact with other AI agents just like portfolio managers would.

And this is a simple demo. Here you would give a little name like Toly Tracker, set up a trigger, and then set up a context, here we're going to read social context. And then we're going to have strategies implemented and after that we'll pay with x402 and USDC on Solana and here this is a My Page where you're able to track the performance of AI agents. And of course, we have a marketplace where you're able to interact with other AI agents to make a better portfolio.

Now, through these efforts we realized that we actually have a missing piece which is an agent authorization layer. This is because users wouldn't want their AI agents to overspend and they don't want to reveal their private information like credit card information. And at the same time it goes same for the providers as well. They don't want to reveal their proprietary information as well as they don't want to mess up with regulators. And we believe the solutions to this is zero knowledge proof tailored towards a non-deterministic nature of AI agents and hardware acceleration techniques that would make these such things scalable.

So if you're aligned with our vision and if you like our thought process, if you're a technical partner, visionary founder, let's connect and let's build the agentic future together. Thank you.

# X402 ecosystem expands as Solana becomes number-one network for payments
Solana has quietly become the busiest chain for x402 payments, clocking an all-time high $380,000 in daily volume and a 750% weekly jump.
Listen0:00
![X402 ecosystem expands as Solana becomes number-one network for payments](https://images.cointelegraph.com/cdn-cgi/image/format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=90,width=1434/https://s3.cointelegraph.com/uploads/2025-12/019ade2d-072b-7f00-91d2-a4dbb1d322f7)
News
COINTELEGRAPH IN YOUR SOCIAL FEED
Solana just logged its biggest week yet for x402, with daily payment volume hitting an all‑time high of about $380,000 on Sunday and about 750% week‑on‑week growth.
The latest activity lifts Solana to the most active network by dollar volume for the transactions, showing how fast the[AI‑agent](https://cointelegraph.com/news/ai-agents-turn-bnb-chain-into-early-entries-and-clean-exits-at-machine-speed)payments narrative is turning into measurable onchain flow.
The rise of x402 payments on Solana marks a turning point for the HTTP-402-based protocol, with pay-per-request stablecoin transfers now flowing through facilitators at a pace that resembles less a speculative spike and more the early contours of machine-driven demand.
Instead of[wash trading or airdrop farming](https://cointelegraph.com/magazine/inside-30000-phone-crypto-airdrop-bot-farm/), bots and agents are beginning to hit Solana for actual services.
![](https://s3.cointelegraph.com/uploads/2025-12/019ade2d-371f-78cf-ab0d-a4d8f608777c)*Source:*[*Solana*](https://x.com/solana/status/1995699607666655472)
While the absolute numbers remain small compared with decentralized finance (DeFi) and trading flows, they offer a clean datapoint that real customers, and not just speculative capital, are starting to settle recurring payments on Solana.
***Related:***[***Why the x402 protocol didn’t fade after the PING hype, and what’s driving the second wave***](https://cointelegraph.com/news/why-the-x402-protocol-didn-t-fade-after-the-ping-hype-and-what-s-driving-the-second-wave)
## Solana: When real usage shows up onchain
What makes this week’s move more striking is that x402 was built for the internet’s back end, not for humans chasing the latest token ticker.
The standard lets[APIs, apps, and AI agents respond](https://cointelegraph.com/explained/coinbases-x402-crypto-payments-over-http-for-ai-and-apis)with a 402 “Payment Required” code, settle a gasless USDC (USDC) transfer on a chain like Solana under the hood, and then serve the content or compute the requester is paying for.
For Solana, becoming the busiest x402 venue strengthens the claim that low fees and high throughput are useful for machine and API micropayments, not just memecoins and high‑frequency trading.
That narrative is starting to show up elsewhere in the stack as well, from developer[guides](https://solana.com/x402/what-is-x402)that walk through x402 integrations on Solana to new[partnerships with the network, like Kalshi,](https://cointelegraph.com/news/kalshi-contracts-tokenized-solana)which aims to push more prediction‑market flows and stablecoin activity onchain, leveraging Solana’s speed.
***Related:***[***AI agents want to handle your crypto wallet, but is it safe?***](https://cointelegraph.com/news/ai-agents-crypto-wallets-safe-risks)

X402 is already being adopted by a growing number of teams in the Web3 space, from protocol extensions that add pri

Today marks an important milestone for Stable and for the broader stablecoin economy. Stable Mainnet is officially live, activating StableChain, our USDT-native Layer 1 designed for high-volume, predictable stablecoin settlement.
This moment represents more than a technical release: it is the beginning of dedicated infrastructure for the stablecoin economy. As we move into this next phase, we want to outline the thesis that guides our work and the role StableChain will play in shaping the future of digital value movement.
Learn more about StableChain:[www.stable.xyz](http://www.stable.xyz)
## **Stable Thesis, Why Stable?**
With Stable Mainnet going live today, we are entering an important moment for both Stable and the broader stablecoin economy. Mainnet marks the point where dedicated stablecoin infrastructure moves from concept to reality, and with it, the opportunity to redefine how stable value moves across the world.
Stablecoins have become one of the world’s most widely adopted digital assets, yet the infrastructure that carries them has not evolved at the pace of their usage. They are used globally, across industries and economies, but they still rely on base layers built for purposes other than payments.
This gap is no longer sustainable.
Stable’s mission is to deliver dedicated payment infrastructure designed around how stablecoins are actually used: to move value quickly, reliably, and with predictable costs. With StableChain, we are advancing that mission: providing a foundation where stablecoin transactions are not secondary activity, but the core function of the system.
Stable Mainnet represents the beginning of this new phase.
**Fast, Secure, Predictable.**
## **Growing Ecosystem of Day-1 Partners**

editorial.headline: Hashed’s ShardLab Deploys x402 Protocol to Power Solana’s 'Agentic Economy'
editorial.standfirst: The VC’s product arm reveals an infrastructure layer that replaces human interfaces with autonomous agent-to-agent settlement, leveraging Coinbase’s x402 standard.
key_takeaways: ShardLab is adapting the HTTP x402 standard and Ethereum's ERC-8004 identity concept for sub-400ms settlements on Solana.
Live demos showcased 'Toly Tracker' (autonomous trading) and a KRW-stablecoin commerce module for purchasing physical goods.
The firm identified a critical market gap for a ZK-powered 'Agent Authorization Layer' to prevent AI overspending and data leaks.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The transition from human-centric web interfaces to an **Agentic Economy**—where AI agents negotiate and settle transactions autonomously—requires a fundamental overhaul of payment rails. **ShardLab**, the product arm of crypto VC heavyweight **Hashed**, argued at Breakpoint that traditional finance is incompatible with this future. According to Product Manager **Jaewook Lee**, AI agents lack legal personhood for bank accounts and cannot operate within the rigid, monthly settlement cycles of legacy systems. The solution lies in blockchain rails that offer instant finality and programmable identity.

ShardLab is operationalizing this thesis by integrating the **x402 protocol** on Solana. Originally developed by Coinbase to revive the dormant HTTP 402 "Payment Required" status code, x402 allows APIs to demand instant stablecoin payments before releasing data or services. ShardLab’s implementation pairs this with an "8004-inspired" reputation module—referencing Ethereum’s **ERC-8004 Trustless Agents** standard—ported to Solana. This combination enables agents to verify each other's credibility and settle transactions in under 400 milliseconds, a speed Lee cites as critical for machine-to-machine commerce.

The firm demonstrated two live implementations of this stack. The first, an "Agentic Payment Module," utilized a **KRW-pegged stablecoin** to allow an AI to search for white Nike sneakers, negotiate SKU data with other agents, and execute a purchase without human input. The second, a trading bot dubbed "Toly Tracker," autonomously managed a portfolio by reading social context and paying for data streams using **USDC** and x402. These use cases signal a shift where stablecoins become the native currency for background API calls rather than just user-facing transfers.

For investors and builders, the "alpha" lies in the infrastructure gap ShardLab identified: an **Agent Authorization Layer**. Lee highlighted that current setups risk AI overspending or leaking proprietary data. ShardLab is actively seeking technical partners to build a solution using **Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKP)** and hardware acceleration. This specific focus suggests that the next wave of VC funding in the agentic space will likely target privacy-preserving control layers that make autonomous agents safe for institutional capital.
